description:
h2. Epic Goal
*As a* platform operator,
*I want* a complete backup/restore/upgrade toolchain with GFS retention and customer-data invariants,
*so that* I can patch and upgrade safely, always recover, and never lose customer data.

h3. Scope
backup/restore/verify scripts (Postgres+Neo4j, GFS retention); full upgrade pipeline with auto-rollback; security-patch with auto-revert; manual rollback; USB air-gap copy; runbooks; CLAUDE.md invariants (rules 12-14).

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{integration/system-scripts.test.ts}}, *when* run, *then* the scripts are covered.
* *Given* an upgrade health-check failure, *then* auto-rollback triggers.
* *Given* any prod state change, *then* a pre-change backup is mandatory (rule 12) and customer data is never dropped (rule 14).

h3. Child Stories
* CRAN-306…CRAN-314

_As-built (Done). Story points + effort aggregate from child stories._

h3. User Story
*As a* platform operator,
*I want* a backup script with GFS retention,
*so that* I have point-in-time DB backups retained on a grandfather-father-son schedule.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{integration/system-scripts.test.ts}}, *then* it's covered.
* *Given* a run, *then* backups land in {{backups/<frequency>/<timestamp>/}}.

h3. Implementation Notes
Postgres (cranis2+forgejo)+Neo4j; 7d/4w/3m/30d-pre-upgrade; {{--pre-upgrade}}/{{--postgres-only}} flags.

h3. Sources
WS1 commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* platform operator,
*I want* an interactive restore with safety backup,
*so that* I can restore confidently without clobbering current state by accident.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* the script, *then* it refuses to run without confirmation and takes a safety backup first.

h3. Implementation Notes
Health-checks after restore.

h3. Sources
WS1 commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* platform operator,
*I want* a weekly backup-verification job,
*so that* silently-broken backups are caught before I need them.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* a valid backup, *then* the script exits 0; otherwise non-zero with explanation.
* *Given* cron, *then* it runs weekly.

h3. Implementation Notes
Spins up temp containers, restores, validates, cleans up.

h3. Sources
WS1 commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* platform operator,
*I want* a full upgrade pipeline with auto-rollback,
*so that* upgrades are pre-flighted, backed up and self-healing on failure.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* a pre-flight failure, *then* the script aborts cleanly.
* *Given* a health-check failure, *then* auto-rollback triggers.

h3. Implementation Notes
pre-flight → backup → pull → build → deploy → health → rollback.

h3. Sources
WS1 commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* platform operator,
*I want* a security-patch script that auto-reverts on test failure,
*so that* I can apply audit fixes safely.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* tests pass, *then* patches stay; *given* tests fail, *then* {{git restore}} reverts.

h3. Implementation Notes
{{npm audit fix}} across all workspaces + test suite.

h3. Sources
WS1 commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* platform operator,
*I want* a manual rollback by code/DB/both,
*so that* I can recover to a known-good point on demand.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* the script, *then* it prompts for scope and performs restore + git reset cleanly.

h3. Implementation Notes
Lists rollback points (backups + git tags).

h3. Sources
WS1 commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* platform operator,
*I want* an air-gapped USB copy of the latest backup,
*so that* I have offline recovery independent of dev/prod.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* the USB volume, *then* it holds the 4 most recent backups, older rotated off.

h3. Implementation Notes


h3. Sources
WS1 commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* platform operator,
*I want* backup/restore and upgrade/patching runbooks,
*so that* the procedures are documented with decision trees.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{docs/backup-and-restore.md}} + {{docs/upgrade-and-patching.md}}, *then* both exist at HEAD.

h3. Implementation Notes


h3. Sources
WS1 commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* AI-assisted maintainer,
*I want* operating invariants codified in CLAUDE.md,
*so that* prod changes are always backup-first and never destroy customer data.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* CLAUDE.md, *then* rules 12 (pre-change backup), 13 (schema-as-code), 14 (customer-data invariant) are present.

h3. Implementation Notes
Backed by the WS1 toolchain.

h3. Sources
Session 62 CLAUDE.md rule commits.

h2. Epic Goal
*As a* security engineer,
*I want* a post-quantum cryptographic foundation across JWTs, encryption and signing,
*so that* the platform resists algorithm-confusion attacks today and Harvest-Now-Decrypt-Later quantum attacks tomorrow.

h3. Scope
HS256 JWT pinning; HKDF key derivation; versioned encryption (v1 legacy / v2 HKDF); Node 24 for native ML-DSA-65; hybrid Ed25519+ML-DSA-65 signing; snapshot signature side-cars; public key endpoint + generator.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{security/pqc-foundation.test.ts}} (25) + {{hybrid-signing.test.ts}} (23) + {{jwt-manipulation.test.ts}}, *then* they pass.
* *Given* a v1 ciphertext, *then* it still decrypts; new writes use v2.

h3. Child Stories
* CRAN-315…CRAN-321

_As-built (Done). Story points + effort aggregate from child stories._

h3. User Story
*As a* security engineer,
*I want* JWT algorithm pinned to HS256,
*so that* alg:none and algorithm-confusion attacks are defeated.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{security/jwt-manipulation.test.ts}}, *then* alg-none / RS256 / HS512 confusion are rejected.

h3. Implementation Notes
Pinned in both sign() and verify().

h3. Sources
WS2 Part 1 commit.

h3. User Story
*As a* security engineer,
*I want* an HKDF key-derivation module,
*so that* keys are domain-separated by purpose from a single master secret.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{security/pqc-foundation.test.ts}}, *then* HKDF outputs match RFC 5869 vectors.

h3. Implementation Notes
{{lib/key-derivation.ts}} HKDF-SHA256; JWT secret derived + per-process cached.

h3. Sources
WS2 Part 1 commit.

h3. User Story
*As a* security engineer,
*I want* versioned encryption with backward-compatible reads,
*so that* encrypted data migrates to HKDF-derived keys without breaking existing ciphertext.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{security/pqc-foundation.test.ts}}, *then* 25 tests pass incl. mixed v1+v2 cohabitation.

h3. Implementation Notes
{{v2:iv:tag:ciphertext}} HKDF-derived; {{v1:...}} auto-detected + decrypted with raw key.

h3. Sources
WS2 Part 1 commit.

h3. User Story
*As a* platform operator,
*I want* Node 24 for native ML-DSA-65 support,
*so that* post-quantum signing uses native OpenSSL, not a WASM polyfill.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* the container, *then* {{node --version}} is 24.x and {{openssl version}} is 3.5+.

h3. Implementation Notes
Dockerfile {{node:22-alpine}}→{{node:24-alpine}}.

h3. Sources
WS2 Part 2 commit.

h3. User Story
*As a* security engineer,
*I want* hybrid Ed25519 + ML-DSA-65 signing,
*so that* signatures are valid classically and post-quantum, with graceful fallback.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{security/hybrid-signing.test.ts}}, *then* 23 tests pass.
* *Given* {{CRANIS2_SIGNING_KEY_MLDSA}} unset, *then* it degrades to Ed25519-only.

h3. Implementation Notes
{{signDocument()}} dual sig; {{verifyHybridSignature()}} AND-logic.

h3. Sources
WS2 Part 2 commit.

h3. User Story
*As a* compliance officer,
*I want* snapshot signature side-cars,
*so that* each snapshot ZIP carries both signatures for independent verification.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* a snapshot ZIP, *then* {{.sig}} + {{.sig.mldsa}} are written beside it.

h3. Implementation Notes
{{compliance-snapshot.ts}} + {{retention-certificate.ts}} carry both.

h3. Sources
WS2 Part 2 commit.

h3. User Story
*As a* third-party verifier,
*I want* a public ML-DSA-65 key endpoint + generator,
*so that* anyone can verify CRANIS2 signatures and operators can mint keys.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{/.well-known/cranis2-signing-key-mldsa.pem}}, *then* it returns a valid PEM.
* *Given* the generator, *then* it produces both key pairs in one run.

h3. Implementation Notes


h3. Sources
WS2 Part 2 commit.

h2. Epic Goal
*As a* security engineer / platform operator,
*I want* the platform hardened and a full key-rotation toolchain,
*so that* the attack surface is minimised and all secrets rotate on a documented cadence.

h3. Scope
DB ports bound to localhost; Forgejo creds in .env + rotated; auth rate limiting (no Retry-After leak); CORS locked to FRONTEND_URL; welcome default creds removed; npm-audit zero; credential/encryption/signing rotation scripts + age checker; rotation runbook.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{security/hardening.test.ts}} (29) + {{key-rotation.test.ts}} (41), *then* they pass.
* *Given* the DB ports, *then* they bind to 127.0.0.1 only.
* *Given* npm audit, *then* all 5 workspaces report 0 vulnerabilities.

h3. Child Stories
* CRAN-322…CRAN-332

_As-built (Done). Story points + effort aggregate from child stories._

h3. User Story
*As a* security engineer,
*I want* database ports bound to localhost only,
*so that* DB/Forgejo containers aren't reachable from public interfaces.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{docker-compose.yml}}, *then* bindings are {{127.0.0.1:5433}}, {{7475}}, {{7688}}, {{3003}}.

h3. Implementation Notes


h3. Sources
WS3 commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* security engineer,
*I want* Forgejo credentials moved to .env and rotated,
*so that* no hardcoded Forgejo credentials remain.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{docker-compose.yml}}, *then* it references {{FORGEJO_*}} env vars with no hardcoded creds.

h3. Implementation Notes


h3. Sources
WS3 commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* security engineer,
*I want* per-category auth rate limiting with no timing leak,
*so that* brute-force is throttled and attackers can't probe the reset window.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{security/hardening.test.ts}}, *then* 29 tests pass.
* *Given* a 429, *then* it has NO {{Retry-After}} header (asserted).

h3. Implementation Notes
login 5/15min, register 3/hr, verify 10/hr, invite 5/hr.

h3. Sources
WS3 commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* security engineer,
*I want* CORS restricted to FRONTEND_URL,
*so that* only the real frontend origin is allowed.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* a pre-flight from another origin, *then* no CORS header is returned.

h3. Implementation Notes
Was previously permissive.

h3. Sources
WS3 commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* security engineer,
*I want* welcome-site default credentials removed,
*so that* no hardcoded admin creds; missing env fails fast.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{grep -r "admin@example" welcome/}}, *then* zero occurrences.
* *Given* a missing required env var, *then* {{requireEnv()}} throws at startup.

h3. Implementation Notes


h3. Sources
WS3 commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* security engineer,
*I want* zero npm-audit vulnerabilities across all workspaces,
*so that* the dependency tree carries no known vulnerabilities.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{npm audit}} per workspace (backend/frontend/e2e/mcp/welcome), *then* each reports 0 at session-60 close.

h3. Implementation Notes
Maintained via the WS1 patch script.

h3. Sources
WS3 + session 59/60 dep-bump commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* platform operator,
*I want* a monthly credential-rotation script,
*so that* DB passwords, JWT secret and welcome secret rotate monthly.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{security/key-rotation.test.ts}}, *then* the rotation flow is covered.

h3. Implementation Notes


h3. Sources
WS3 Part 2 commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* platform operator,
*I want* an annual lab-based encryption-key rotation,
*so that* the master key rotates without touching prod directly.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* the script, *then* re-encryption runs against a backup of prod, not prod.

h3. Implementation Notes
Decrypts+re-encrypts PATs on the lab server; deploy in maintenance window.

h3. Sources
WS3 Part 2 commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* platform operator,
*I want* a prod-side script to apply a rotation package,
*so that* rotation prepared in the lab is applied safely in the maintenance window.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* an invalid package signature, *then* the script aborts cleanly.

h3. Implementation Notes


h3. Sources
WS3 Part 2 commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* platform operator,
*I want* signing-key rotation plus an overdue-rotation checker,
*so that* signing keys rotate annually and overdue rotations are flagged.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{security/key-rotation.test.ts}}, *then* 41 tests pass.
* *Given* a weekly cron, *then* {{check-rotation-age.sh}} warns when a rotation is overdue.

h3. Implementation Notes
{{rotate-signing-keys.sh}} archives old public keys for historical verification.

h3. Sources
WS3 Part 2 commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* platform operator,
*I want* a key-rotation runbook,
*so that* all rotations are documented with the HNDL threat context.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{docs/key-rotation.md}}, *then* it exists at HEAD.

h3. Implementation Notes
Harvest-Now-Decrypt-Later context.

h3. Sources
WS3 Part 2 docs commit.
